title: Actinoseris polyphylla
intro: Actinoseris polyphylla is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as a shrubby perennial herb native to the Andean region. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it a potential treatment for wounds and infections. The bioactive constituents of Actinoseris polyphylla include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Actinoseris polyphylla is often used to make infusions, teas, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments, including skin conditions and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Actinoseris polyphylla is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. More specifically, it is placed within the subfamily Vernonioideae and the tribe Vernonieae. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics and evolutionary relationships with other species. The genus Actinoseris is a monotypic genus, meaning it contains only one species. This classification is subject to revision as new research and discoveries become available.
